side_effects.description: NAD+ and GHK-Cu both described as spicy (painful); GHK-Cu too spicy for abdominal injections

protocol_notes: Successfully manages NAD+ pH by adding sodium bicarbonate Hospira BAC water; reports pH on compounds should be 5-6 but has received vendors sending pH 3-4; can inject NAD+ in tummy and thighs with no issue if pH is corrected; injects GHK-Cu (KLOW) in glute with no issues; recommends zinc and antihistamine for GHK-Cu; uses pH strips to verify pH before injection
